pattern: Mutual reachability under the jump rules collapses the array into contiguous blocks, so the answer for every index is its block maximum.
hints: Think about which indices are mutually reachable rather than tracing each starting point separately.
Two positions end up in the same group exactly when some earlier element is larger than a later one spanning them, and these groups turn out to be contiguous index ranges.
A boundary between i and i+1 exists only when the maximum of the prefix up to i is at most the minimum of the suffix from i+1; split on those boundaries and assign each block its largest value.

edge_inputs.description: plateau of equal values does not connect
max in the middle reached from both sides

stress.description: random array over a small value range to create many merges and plateaus
